steps.title: Step 1 — clear the fractions
steps.body: Start from $\dfrac{5x+7}{(x+1)(x+2)} \equiv \dfrac{A}{x+1} + \dfrac{B}{x+2}$. Multiply every term by $(x+1)(x+2)$ to remove all denominators: $5x+7 \equiv A(x+2) + B(x+1)$.

steps.title: Step 2 — substitute a value that removes B
steps.body: This identity holds for every value of $x$, so choose $x=-1$ (the root of the $(x+1)$ bracket) to make the $B$ term vanish: $5(-1)+7 = A(-1+2) + B(0) \Rightarrow 2 = A(1) \Rightarrow A = 2$.

steps.title: Step 3 — substitute a value that removes A
steps.body: Now choose $x=-2$ (the root of the $(x+2)$ bracket) to make the $A$ term vanish: $5(-2)+7 = A(0) + B(-2+1) \Rightarrow -3 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B = 3$.

steps.title: Step 4 — write and check the result
steps.body: So $\dfrac{5x+7}{(x+1)(x+2)} \equiv \dfrac{2}{x+1} + \dfrac{3}{x+2}$. **Check** by recombining: $\dfrac{2(x+2)+3(x+1)}{(x+1)(x+2)} = \dfrac{2x+4+3x+3}{(x+1)(x+2)} = \dfrac{5x+7}{(x+1)(x+2)}$ ✓ — matches the original fraction exactly.
